server.c(3) Library Functions Manual server.c(3)

NAME

server.c

SYNOPSIS

Functions


asciichat_error_t acds_server_init (acds_server_t *server, const acds_config_t *config)
Initialize discovery server. asciichat_error_t acds_server_run (acds_server_t *server)
Run discovery server main loop. void acds_server_shutdown (acds_server_t *server)
Shutdown discovery server. void * acds_client_handler (void *arg)
Per-client connection handler (thread entry point). void * acds_websocket_client_handler (void *arg)
WebSocket client handler (thread entry point).

Function Documentation

void * acds_client_handler (void * arg)

Per-client connection handler (thread entry point). Processes ACIP packets from a connected client. Handles crypto handshake, then dispatches packets to session/signaling handlers.

Parameters

arg Pointer to client connection context

Returns

NULL (thread exit value)

Definition at line 945 of file src/discovery-service/server.c.

945                                      {
946   tcp_client_context_t *ctx = (tcp_client_context_t *)arg;
947   if (!ctx) {
948     log_error("Client handler: NULL context");
949     return NULL;
950   }
951 
952   acds_server_t *server = (acds_server_t *)ctx->user_data;
953   socket_t client_socket = ctx->client_socket;
954 
955   // Get client IP for logging
956   char client_ip[INET6_ADDRSTRLEN] = {0};
957   tcp_client_context_get_ip(ctx, client_ip, sizeof(client_ip));
958 
959   log_info("Client handler started for %s", client_ip);
960 
961   // Register client in TCP server registry with allocated client data
962   acds_client_data_t *client_data = SAFE_MALLOC(sizeof(acds_client_data_t), acds_client_data_t *);
963   if (!client_data) {
964     tcp_server_reject_client(client_socket, "Failed to allocate client data");
965     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
966     return NULL;
967   }
968   memset(client_data, 0, sizeof(*client_data));
969   client_data->joined_session = false;
970   client_data->handshake_complete = false;
971 
972   // Initialize crypto handshake context with client IP name
973   char crypto_name[96];
974   SAFE_SNPRINTF(crypto_name, sizeof(crypto_name), "crypto_discovery_client_%s", client_ip);
975   asciichat_error_t handshake_result = crypto_handshake_init(crypto_name, &client_data->handshake_ctx, true);
976   if (handshake_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
977     log_error("Failed to initialize crypto handshake for client %s", client_ip);
978     SAFE_FREE(client_data);
979     tcp_server_reject_client(client_socket, "Failed to initialize crypto handshake");
980     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
981     return NULL;
982   }
983 
984   // Set server identity keys for handshake
985   client_data->handshake_ctx.server_public_key.type = KEY_TYPE_ED25519;
986   client_data->handshake_ctx.server_private_key.type = KEY_TYPE_ED25519;
987   memcpy(client_data->handshake_ctx.server_public_key.key, server->identity_public, 32);
988   memcpy(client_data->handshake_ctx.server_private_key.key.ed25519, server->identity_secret, 64);
989 
990   if (tcp_server_add_client(&server->tcp_server, client_socket, client_data) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
991     SAFE_FREE(client_data);
992     tcp_server_reject_client(client_socket, "Failed to register client in registry");
993     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
994     return NULL;
995   }
996 
997   log_debug("Client %s registered (socket=%d, total=%zu)", client_ip, client_socket,
998             tcp_server_get_client_count(&server->tcp_server));
999 
1000   // Create transport for handshake and subsequent client communication
1001   acip_transport_t *transport = acip_tcp_transport_create("acds_tcp_client", client_socket, NULL);
1002   if (!transport) {
1003     log_error("Failed to create TCP transport for client %s", client_ip);
1004     tcp_server_remove_client(&server->tcp_server, client_socket);
1005     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
1006     return NULL;
1007   }
1008 
1009   // Perform crypto handshake (three-step process)
1010   log_debug("Performing crypto handshake with client %s", client_ip);
1011 
1012   // Step -1: Receive and discard protocol version from client
1013   {
1014     packet_type_t pkt_type;
1015     void *pkt_data = NULL;
1016     size_t pkt_len = 0;
1017     void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1018 
1019     handshake_result = packet_receive_via_transport(transport, &pkt_type, &pkt_data, &pkt_len, &alloc_buffer);
1020     if (handshake_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1021       log_warn("Failed to receive PROTOCOL_VERSION from client %s", client_ip);
1022       acip_transport_destroy(transport);
1023       tcp_server_remove_client(&server->tcp_server, client_socket);
1024       SAFE_FREE(ctx);
1025       return NULL;
1026     }
1027 
1028     if (pkt_type != PACKET_TYPE_PROTOCOL_VERSION) {
1029       log_warn("Expected PROTOCOL_VERSION, got packet type %u from client %s", pkt_type, client_ip);
1030       buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1031       acip_transport_destroy(transport);
1032       tcp_server_remove_client(&server->tcp_server, client_socket);
1033       SAFE_FREE(ctx);
1034       return NULL;
1035     }
1036 
1037     buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1038     log_debug("Received PROTOCOL_VERSION from client %s", client_ip);
1039   }
1040 
1041   // Step 0: Send crypto parameters
1042   handshake_result = crypto_handshake_server_send_parameters(&client_data->handshake_ctx, transport);
1043   if (handshake_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1044     log_warn("Crypto parameters send failed for client %s", client_ip);
1045     acip_transport_destroy(transport);
1046     tcp_server_remove_client(&server->tcp_server, client_socket);
1047     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
1048     return NULL;
1049   }
1050 
1051   // Step 1: Start handshake (send server key, receive client key)
1052   handshake_result = crypto_handshake_server_start(&client_data->handshake_ctx, transport);
1053   if (handshake_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1054     log_warn("Crypto handshake start failed for client %s", client_ip);
1055     acip_transport_destroy(transport);
1056     tcp_server_remove_client(&server->tcp_server, client_socket);
1057     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
1058     return NULL;
1059   }
1060 
1061   // Step 2: Authentication challenge (if required)
1062   {
1063     packet_type_t packet_type;
1064     uint8_t *payload = NULL;
1065     size_t payload_len = 0;
1066     int recv_result = receive_packet(client_socket, &packet_type, (void **)&payload, &payload_len);
1067     if (recv_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1068       log_warn("Failed to receive KEY_EXCHANGE_RESPONSE from client %s", client_ip);
1069       acip_transport_destroy(transport);
1070       tcp_server_remove_client(&server->tcp_server, client_socket);
1071       SAFE_FREE(ctx);
1072       return NULL;
1073     }
1074     handshake_result = crypto_handshake_server_auth_challenge(&client_data->handshake_ctx, transport, packet_type,
1075                                                               payload, payload_len);
1076     buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
1077   }
1078   if (handshake_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1079     log_warn("Crypto handshake auth challenge failed for client %s", client_ip);
1080     acip_transport_destroy(transport);
1081     tcp_server_remove_client(&server->tcp_server, client_socket);
1082     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
1083     return NULL;
1084   }
1085 
1086   // Step 3: Complete handshake (verify and finalize) - skip if already complete
1087   if (client_data->handshake_ctx.state != CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_READY) {
1088     packet_type_t packet_type = 0;
1089     uint8_t *payload = NULL;
1090     size_t payload_len = 0;
1091     int recv_result = receive_packet(client_socket, &packet_type, (void **)&payload, &payload_len);
1092     if (recv_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1093       log_warn("Failed to receive AUTH_RESPONSE from client %s", client_ip);
1094       acip_transport_destroy(transport);
1095       tcp_server_remove_client(&server->tcp_server, client_socket);
1096       SAFE_FREE(ctx);
1097       return NULL;
1098     }
1099     handshake_result =
1100         crypto_handshake_server_complete(&client_data->handshake_ctx, transport, packet_type, payload, payload_len);
1101     buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
1102     if (handshake_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1103       log_warn("Crypto handshake complete failed for client %s", client_ip);
1104       acip_transport_destroy(transport);
1105       tcp_server_remove_client(&server->tcp_server, client_socket);
1106       SAFE_FREE(ctx);
1107       return NULL;
1108     }
1109   } else {
1110     log_debug("Crypto handshake already complete from auth_challenge step for client %s", client_ip);
1111   }
1112 
1113   client_data->handshake_complete = true;
1114   log_info("Crypto handshake complete for client %s", client_ip);
1115 
1116   // Store transport and registry ID in client data, and link back from transport
1117   client_data->transport = transport;
1118   client_data->registry_id = client_socket;
1119   transport->user_data = client_data;
1120 
1121   // Main packet processing loop
1122   uint64_t last_packet_time_ns = time_get_ns();
1123   const uint64_t idle_disconnect_ns = 60ULL * NS_PER_SEC_INT; // Disconnect after 60s idle
1124 
1125   while (atomic_load_bool(&server->tcp_server.running)) {
1126     packet_type_t packet_type;
1127     void *payload = NULL;
1128     size_t payload_size = 0;
1129 
1130     // Receive packet (blocking with system timeout)
1131     int result = receive_packet(client_socket, &packet_type, &payload, &payload_size);
1132 
1133     if (result >= 0) {
1134       last_packet_time_ns = time_get_ns();
1135       log_info("★ ACDS_CLIENT_HANDLER: Received packet type=%u from %s, payload_size=%zu", packet_type, client_ip,
1136                payload_size);
1137 
1138       // Track network stats (TCP packets)
1139       // Packet size includes header (6 bytes: magic 4 + type 1 + length 2 + CRC 4)
1140       size_t total_packet_size = payload_size + 14; // 4 (magic) + 1 (type) + 2 (length) + 4 (CRC) + 3 (client ID)
1141       atomic_fetch_add_u64(&server->stats.bytes_received, total_packet_size);
1142       atomic_fetch_add_u64(&server->stats.packets_received, 1);
1143     }
1144 
1145     if (result < 0) {
1146       // Check error context to distinguish timeout from actual disconnect
1147       asciichat_error_context_t err_ctx;
1148       bool has_context = HAS_ERRNO(&err_ctx);
1149 
1150       // Check if this is a timeout (non-fatal) or actual disconnect (fatal)
1151       asciichat_error_t error = GET_ERRNO();
1152       if (error == ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT ||
1153           (error == ERROR_NETWORK && has_context && strstr(err_ctx.context_message, "timed out") != NULL)) {
1154         // Check if client has been idle too long (abrupt disconnect without FIN)
1155         uint64_t idle_ns = time_get_ns() - last_packet_time_ns;
1156         if (idle_ns >= idle_disconnect_ns) {
1157           log_info("Client %s: idle for %llu seconds, disconnecting", client_ip,
1158                    (unsigned long long)(idle_ns / NS_PER_SEC_INT));
1159           if (payload) {
1160             buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_size);
1161           }
1162           break;
1163         }
1164         log_debug("Client %s: receive timeout, continuing to wait for packets", client_ip);
1165         if (payload) {
1166           buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_size);
1167         }
1168         continue;
1169       }
1170 
1171       // Actual disconnect or fatal error
1172       log_info("Client %s disconnected", client_ip);
1173       if (payload) {
1174         buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_size);
1175       }
1176       break;
1177     }
1178 
1179     log_debug("Received packet type 0x%02X from %s, length=%zu", packet_type, client_ip, payload_size);
1180 
1181     // Multi-key session creation protocol: block non-PING/PONG/SESSION_CREATE messages
1182     if (client_data->in_multikey_session_create) {
1183       bool allowed = (packet_type == PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_SESSION_CREATE || packet_type == PACKET_TYPE_PING ||
1184                       packet_type == PACKET_TYPE_PONG);
1185 
1186       if (!allowed) {
1187         log_warn("Client %s sent packet type 0x%02X during multi-key session creation - only SESSION_CREATE/PING/PONG "
1188                  "allowed",
1189                  client_ip, packet_type);
1190 
1191         // Send error response using persistent transport
1192         acip_send_error(transport, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
1193                         "Only SESSION_CREATE/PING/PONG allowed during multi-key session creation");
1194 
1195         // Free payload and continue
1196         if (payload) {
1197           buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_size);
1198         }
1199         continue;
1200       }
1201     }
1202 
1203     // O(1) ACIP array-based dispatch
1204     // Set server context for callbacks
1205     acip_acds_callbacks_t callbacks = g_acds_callbacks;
1206     callbacks.app_ctx = server;
1207 
1208     asciichat_error_t dispatch_result =
1209         acip_handle_acds_packet(transport, packet_type, payload, payload_size, client_ip, &callbacks);
1210 
1211     if (dispatch_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1212       log_warn("ACIP handler failed for packet type 0x%02X from %s: %s", packet_type, client_ip,
1213                asciichat_error_string(dispatch_result));
1214     }
1215 
1216     // Free payload (allocated by receive_packet via buffer_pool_alloc)
1217     if (payload) {
1218       buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_size);
1219     }
1220 
1221     // Keep handler running after session_established
1222     // Handler continues to receive and relay WebRTC signaling packets (SDP, ICE)
1223     // Broadcasts don't go through this socket - they use tcp_server_foreach_client() to write directly
1224   }
1225 
1226   // Cleanup - handler always cleans up since it runs until connection closes
1227   tcp_server_remove_client(&server->tcp_server, client_socket);
1228   log_debug("Client %s unregistered (total=%zu)", client_ip, tcp_server_get_client_count(&server->tcp_server));
1229 
1230   // Destroy transport before closing socket
1231   acip_transport_destroy(transport);
1232   socket_close(client_socket);
1233 
1234   SAFE_FREE(ctx);
1235 
1236   log_info("Client handler finished for %s", client_ip);
1237   return NULL;
1238 }

Referenced by acds_server_init().






asciichat_error_t acds_server_init (acds_server_t *
  server, const acds_config_t * config)


Initialize discovery server. Loads or generates identity keys,
    opens database, creates session registry, and binds TCP socket.




Parameters


server Server structure to initialize


Config Module Configuration from command-line
  parsing


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code otherwise


Definition at line 193 of file
    src/discovery-service/server.c.


193                                                                                        {
194   if (!server || !config) {
195     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "server or config is NULL");
196   }
197 
198   memset(server, 0, sizeof(*server));
199   memcpy(&server->config, config, sizeof(acds_config_t));
200 
201   // Initialize stats
202   atomic_store_u64(&server->stats.bytes_sent, 0);
203   atomic_store_u64(&server->stats.bytes_received, 0);
204   atomic_store_u64(&server->stats.packets_sent, 0);
205   atomic_store_u64(&server->stats.packets_received, 0);
206   server->stats.start_time = time(NULL);
207 
208   // Open database (SQLite as single source of truth)
209   asciichat_error_t result = database_init(config->database_path, &server->db);
210   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
211     return result;
212   }
213 
214   // Initialize rate limiter with SQLite backend
215   server->rate_limiter = rate_limiter_create_sqlite(NULL); // NULL = externally managed DB
216   if (!server->rate_limiter) {
217     database_close(server->db);
218     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create rate limiter");
219   }
220 
221   // Set the database handle for the rate limiter
222   rate_limiter_set_sqlite_db(server->rate_limiter, server->db);
223 
224   // Configure TCP server
225   tcp_server_config_t tcp_config = {
226       .port = config->port,
227       .ipv4_address = (config->address[0] != '\0') ? config->address : NULL,
228       .ipv6_address = (config->address6[0] != '\0') ? config->address6 : NULL,
229       .bind_ipv4 = (config->address[0] != '\0') || (config->address[0] == '\0' && config->address6[0] == '\0'),
230       .bind_ipv6 = (config->address6[0] != '\0') || (config->address[0] == '\0' && config->address6[0] == '\0'),
231       .accept_timeout_sec = 1,
232       .client_handler = acds_client_handler,
233       .user_data = server,
234   };
235 
236   // Initialize TCP server
237   result = tcp_server_init(&server->tcp_server, &tcp_config);
238   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
239     rate_limiter_destroy(server->rate_limiter);
240     database_close(server->db);
241     return result;
242   }
243 
244   // Initialize background worker thread pool
245   atomic_store_bool(&server->shutdown, false);
246   server->worker_pool = thread_pool_create("acds_workers");
247   if (!server->worker_pool) {
248     log_warn("Failed to create worker thread pool");
249     tcp_server_destroy(&server->tcp_server);
250     rate_limiter_destroy(server->rate_limiter);
251     database_close(server->db);
252     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create worker thread pool");
253   }
254 
255   // Spawn cleanup thread in worker pool
256   if (thread_pool_spawn(server->worker_pool, cleanup_thread_func, server, 0, "cleanup") != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
257     log_warn("Failed to spawn cleanup thread (continuing without cleanup)");
258   }
259 
260   log_info("Discovery server initialized successfully");
261   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
262 }

    acds_config_t::address, acds_config_t::address6,
    ASCIICHAT_OK, atomic_store_bool(), atomic_store_u64(),
    acds_stats_t::bytes_received, acds_stats_t::bytes_sent,
    acds_server_t::config, database_close(),
    database_init(), acds_config_t::database_path,
    acds_server_t::db, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, ERROR_MEMORY,
    log_info, log_warn, acds_stats_t::packets_received,
    acds_stats_t::packets_sent, acds_config_t::port,
    acds_server_t::rate_limiter, rate_limiter_create_sqlite(),
    rate_limiter_destroy(), rate_limiter_set_sqlite_db(),
    SET_ERRNO, acds_server_t::shutdown,
    acds_stats_t::start_time, acds_server_t::stats,
    acds_server_t::tcp_server, tcp_server_destroy(),
    tcp_server_init(), thread_pool_create(),

Referenced by acds_main().






asciichat_error_t acds_server_run (acds_server_t *
  server)


Run discovery server main loop. Accepts client connections and
    spawns handler threads. Blocks until shutdown signal received.




Parameters


server Initialized server structure


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code otherwise


Definition at line 264 of file
    src/discovery-service/server.c.


264                                                          {
265   if (!server) {
266     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "server is NULL");
267   }
268 
269   log_info("Discovery server accepting connections on port %d", server->config.port);
270 
271   // Delegate to TCP server abstraction
272   return tcp_server_run(&server->tcp_server);
273 }






References acds_server_t::config,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, log_info, acds_config_t::port,
    SET_ERRNO, acds_server_t::tcp_server, and
    tcp_server_run().


Referenced by acds_main().






void
  acds_server_shutdown (acds_server_t * server)


Shutdown discovery server. Closes listen socket, stops accepting
    connections, waits for handler threads to exit, closes database, and frees
    resources.




Parameters


server Server structure to clean up


Definition at line 275 of file
    src/discovery-service/server.c.


275                                                  {
276   if (!server) {
277     return;
278   }
279 
280   // Signal shutdown to worker threads
281   atomic_store_bool(&server->shutdown, true);
282 
283   // Shutdown TCP server (closes listen sockets, stops accept loop)
284   tcp_server_destroy(&server->tcp_server);
285 
286   // Wait for all client handler threads to exit
287   size_t remaining_clients;
288   int shutdown_attempts = 0;
289   const int max_shutdown_attempts = 100; // 10 seconds (100 * 100ms)
290 
291   while ((remaining_clients = tcp_server_get_client_count(&server->tcp_server)) > 0 &&
292          shutdown_attempts < max_shutdown_attempts) {
293     log_debug("Waiting for %zu client handler threads to exit (attempt %d/%d)", remaining_clients,
294               shutdown_attempts + 1, max_shutdown_attempts);
295     APP_CALLBACK_VOID(platform_pump_events);
296     platform_sleep_ms(100);
297     shutdown_attempts++;
298   }
299 
300   if (remaining_clients > 0) {
301     log_warn("Server shutdown: %zu client handler threads still running after 10 seconds", remaining_clients);
302   } else if (shutdown_attempts > 0) {
303     log_debug("All client handler threads exited gracefully");
304   }
305 
306   // Stop and destroy worker thread pool (cleanup thread, etc.)
307   if (server->worker_pool) {
308     thread_pool_destroy(server->worker_pool);
309     server->worker_pool = NULL;
310     log_debug("Worker thread pool stopped");
311   }
312 
313   // Destroy rate limiter
314   if (server->rate_limiter) {
315     rate_limiter_destroy(server->rate_limiter);
316     server->rate_limiter = NULL;
317   }
318 
319   // Close database
320   if (server->db) {
321     database_close(server->db);
322     server->db = NULL;
323   }
324 
325   log_info("Server shutdown complete");
326 }






References APP_CALLBACK_VOID, atomic_store_bool(),
    database_close(), acds_server_t::db, log_debug,
    log_info, log_warn, platform_sleep_ms(),
    acds_server_t::rate_limiter, rate_limiter_destroy(),
    acds_server_t::shutdown, acds_server_t::tcp_server,
    tcp_server_destroy(), tcp_server_get_client_count(),
    thread_pool_destroy(), and acds_server_t::worker_pool.


Referenced by acds_main().






void
  * acds_websocket_client_handler (void * arg)


WebSocket client handler (thread entry point). Processes ACIP
    packets from a WebSocket client connection. Similar to TCP handler but uses
    transport-based APIs for crypto handshake.




Parameters


arg Pointer to
  websocket_client_context_t


Returns


NULL (thread exit value)


Definition at line 1244 of file
    src/discovery-service/server.c.


1244                                                {
1245   websocket_client_context_t *ctx = (websocket_client_context_t *)arg;
1246   if (!ctx) {
1247     log_error("WebSocket client handler: NULL context");
1248     return NULL;
1249   }
1250 
1251   acds_server_t *server = (acds_server_t *)ctx->user_data;
1252   acip_transport_t *transport = ctx->transport;
1253   char *client_ip = ctx->client_ip;
1254 
1255   log_info("WebSocket client handler started for %s", client_ip);
1256 
1257   // Allocate client_data
1258   acds_client_data_t *client_data = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(acds_client_data_t), acds_client_data_t *);
1259   if (!client_data) {
1260     log_error("Failed to allocate client data for WebSocket client %s", client_ip);
1261     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
1262     return NULL;
1263   }
1264 
1265   client_data->transport = transport;
1266   client_data->joined_session = false;
1267   client_data->handshake_complete = false;
1268   transport->user_data = client_data;
1269 
1270   // Initialize crypto handshake context with client IP name
1271   char crypto_name[96];
1272   SAFE_SNPRINTF(crypto_name, sizeof(crypto_name), "crypto_discovery_websocket_%s", client_ip);
1273   asciichat_error_t hs_init_result = crypto_handshake_init(crypto_name, &client_data->handshake_ctx, true);
1274   if (hs_init_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1275     log_error("Failed to initialize crypto handshake for WebSocket client %s", client_ip);
1276     SAFE_FREE(client_data);
1277     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
1278     return NULL;
1279   }
1280 
1281   // Set server identity keys for handshake
1282   client_data->handshake_ctx.server_public_key.type = KEY_TYPE_ED25519;
1283   client_data->handshake_ctx.server_private_key.type = KEY_TYPE_ED25519;
1284   memcpy(client_data->handshake_ctx.server_public_key.key, server->identity_public, 32);
1285   memcpy(client_data->handshake_ctx.server_private_key.key.ed25519, server->identity_secret, 64);
1286 
1287   // Generate synthetic socket ID for tcp_server registry
1288   static _Atomic int g_ws_synthetic_id = INT_MAX;
1289   int synthetic_id = atomic_fetch_sub(&g_ws_synthetic_id, 1);
1290   client_data->registry_id = synthetic_id;
1291 
1292   // Register in tcp_server with synthetic ID (so callbacks can find client_data)
1293   if (tcp_server_add_client(&server->tcp_server, synthetic_id, client_data) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1294     log_error("Failed to register WebSocket client %s in registry", client_ip);
1295     SAFE_FREE(client_data);
1296     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
1297     return NULL;
1298   }
1299 
1300   log_debug("WebSocket client %s registered (synthetic_id=%d, total=%zu)", client_ip, synthetic_id,
1301             tcp_server_get_client_count(&server->tcp_server));
1302 
1303   // Determine if client authentication is required by checking server configuration
1304   bool auth_required = server->config.require_client_identity;
1305   ctx->auth_required = auth_required;
1306 
1307   // Skip crypto handshake only for wss:// connections WITHOUT authentication requirement
1308   // For plain ws://, always do crypto handshake
1309   // For wss:// WITH auth requirement, always do crypto handshake
1310   bool skip_handshake = ctx->is_secure && !auth_required;
1311 
1312   if (skip_handshake) {
1313     client_data->handshake_complete = true;
1314     log_info("WebSocket connection from %s - skipping crypto handshake (TLS encryption used, no auth required)",
1315              client_ip);
1316   } else {
1317     log_info("WebSocket connection from %s - proceeding with crypto handshake%s", client_ip,
1318              auth_required ? " (client authentication required)" : "");
1319 
1320     // Perform crypto handshake (three-step process)
1321     log_debug("Performing crypto handshake with WebSocket client %s", client_ip);
1322 
1323     // Step -1: Receive and discard protocol version from client
1324     {
1325       packet_type_t pkt_type;
1326       void *pkt_data = NULL;
1327       size_t pkt_len = 0;
1328       void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1329 
1330       asciichat_error_t hs_result =
1331           packet_receive_via_transport(transport, &pkt_type, &pkt_data, &pkt_len, &alloc_buffer);
1332       if (hs_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1333         log_warn("Failed to receive PROTOCOL_VERSION from WebSocket client %s", client_ip);
1334         buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1335         tcp_server_remove_client(&server->tcp_server, synthetic_id);
1336         SAFE_FREE(ctx);
1337         return NULL;
1338       }
1339 
1340       if (pkt_type != PACKET_TYPE_PROTOCOL_VERSION) {
1341         log_warn("Expected PROTOCOL_VERSION, got packet type %u from WebSocket client %s", pkt_type, client_ip);
1342         buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1343         tcp_server_remove_client(&server->tcp_server, synthetic_id);
1344         SAFE_FREE(ctx);
1345         return NULL;
1346       }
1347 
1348       buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1349       log_debug("Received PROTOCOL_VERSION from WebSocket client %s", client_ip);
1350     }
1351 
1352     // Step 0: Send crypto parameters
1353     asciichat_error_t hs_result = crypto_handshake_server_send_parameters(&client_data->handshake_ctx, transport);
1354     if (hs_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1355       log_warn("Crypto parameters send failed for WebSocket client %s", client_ip);
1356       tcp_server_remove_client(&server->tcp_server, synthetic_id);
1357       SAFE_FREE(ctx);
1358       return NULL;
1359     }
1360 
1361     // Step 1: Start handshake (send server key, receive client key)
1362     hs_result = crypto_handshake_server_start(&client_data->handshake_ctx, transport);
1363     if (hs_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1364       log_warn("Crypto handshake start failed for WebSocket client %s", client_ip);
1365       tcp_server_remove_client(&server->tcp_server, synthetic_id);
1366       SAFE_FREE(ctx);
1367       return NULL;
1368     }
1369 
1370     // Step 2: Authentication challenge (if required)
1371     {
1372       packet_type_t pkt_type;
1373       void *pkt_data = NULL;
1374       size_t pkt_len = 0;
1375       void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1376 
1377       hs_result = packet_receive_via_transport(transport, &pkt_type, &pkt_data, &pkt_len, &alloc_buffer);
1378       if (hs_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1379         log_warn("Failed to receive KEY_EXCHANGE_RESPONSE from WebSocket client %s", client_ip);
1380         buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1381         tcp_server_remove_client(&server->tcp_server, synthetic_id);
1382         SAFE_FREE(ctx);
1383         return NULL;
1384       }
1385 
1386       hs_result =
1387           crypto_handshake_server_auth_challenge(&client_data->handshake_ctx, transport, pkt_type, pkt_data, pkt_len);
1388       buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1389     }
1390     if (hs_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1391       log_warn("Crypto handshake auth challenge failed for WebSocket client %s", client_ip);
1392       tcp_server_remove_client(&server->tcp_server, synthetic_id);
1393       SAFE_FREE(ctx);
1394       return NULL;
1395     }
1396 
1397     // Step 3: Complete handshake (verify and finalize) - skip if already complete
1398     if (client_data->handshake_ctx.state != CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_READY) {
1399       packet_type_t pkt_type = 0;
1400       void *pkt_data = NULL;
1401       size_t pkt_len = 0;
1402       void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1403 
1404       hs_result = packet_receive_via_transport(transport, &pkt_type, &pkt_data, &pkt_len, &alloc_buffer);
1405       if (hs_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1406         log_warn("Failed to receive AUTH_RESPONSE from WebSocket client %s", client_ip);
1407         buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1408         tcp_server_remove_client(&server->tcp_server, synthetic_id);
1409         SAFE_FREE(ctx);
1410         return NULL;
1411       }
1412 
1413       hs_result = crypto_handshake_server_complete(&client_data->handshake_ctx, transport, pkt_type, pkt_data, pkt_len);
1414       buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1415       if (hs_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1416         log_warn("Crypto handshake complete failed for WebSocket client %s", client_ip);
1417         tcp_server_remove_client(&server->tcp_server, synthetic_id);
1418         SAFE_FREE(ctx);
1419         return NULL;
1420       }
1421     }
1422 
1423     client_data->handshake_complete = true;
1424     log_debug("Crypto handshake completed for WebSocket client %s", client_ip);
1425   }
1426 
1427   // Main packet processing loop using transport recv
1428   while (atomic_load_bool(&server->tcp_server.running)) {
1429     void *recv_buffer = NULL;
1430     size_t recv_len = 0;
1431     void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1432 
1433     asciichat_error_t recv_result = acip_transport_recv(transport, &recv_buffer, &recv_len, &alloc_buffer);
1434     if (recv_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1435       // Check if this is a timeout or disconnect
1436       if (recv_result == ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT) {
1437         log_debug("WebSocket client %s: receive timeout, continuing to wait for packets", client_ip);
1438         if (alloc_buffer) {
1439           buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1440         }
1441         continue;
1442       }
1443 
1444       // Actual disconnect or fatal error
1445       log_info("WebSocket client %s disconnected", client_ip);
1446       if (alloc_buffer) {
1447         buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1448       }
1449       break;
1450     }
1451 
1452     // Parse packet header from received data
1453     if (recv_len < sizeof(packet_header_t)) {
1454       log_warn("WebSocket client %s: received packet too small (%zu bytes)", client_ip, recv_len);
1455       if (alloc_buffer) {
1456         buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1457       }
1458       continue;
1459     }
1460 
1461     const packet_header_t *header = (const packet_header_t *)recv_buffer;
1462     packet_type_t packet_type = (packet_type_t)NET_TO_HOST_U16(header->type);
1463     size_t payload_size = NET_TO_HOST_U32(header->length);
1464     const void *payload = (const uint8_t *)recv_buffer + sizeof(packet_header_t);
1465 
1466     // Validate payload size
1467     if (recv_len < sizeof(packet_header_t) + payload_size) {
1468       log_warn("WebSocket client %s: packet size mismatch (expected %zu, got %zu)", client_ip,
1469                sizeof(packet_header_t) + payload_size, recv_len);
1470       if (alloc_buffer) {
1471         buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1472       }
1473       continue;
1474     }
1475 
1476     log_debug("Received packet type 0x%02X from WebSocket client %s, length=%zu", packet_type, client_ip, payload_size);
1477 
1478     // Multi-key session creation protocol: block non-PING/PONG/SESSION_CREATE messages
1479     if (client_data->in_multikey_session_create) {
1480       bool allowed = (packet_type == PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_SESSION_CREATE || packet_type == PACKET_TYPE_PING ||
1481                       packet_type == PACKET_TYPE_PONG);
1482 
1483       if (!allowed) {
1484         log_warn("WebSocket client %s sent packet type 0x%02X during multi-key session creation - only "
1485                  "SESSION_CREATE/PING/PONG allowed",
1486                  client_ip, packet_type);
1487         acip_send_error(transport, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
1488                         "Only SESSION_CREATE/PING/PONG allowed during multi-key session creation");
1489         if (alloc_buffer) {
1490           buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1491         }
1492         continue;
1493       }
1494     }
1495 
1496     // O(1) ACIP array-based dispatch
1497     acip_acds_callbacks_t callbacks = g_acds_callbacks;
1498     callbacks.app_ctx = server;
1499 
1500     asciichat_error_t dispatch_result =
1501         acip_handle_acds_packet(transport, packet_type, payload, payload_size, client_ip, &callbacks);
1502 
1503     if (dispatch_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1504       log_warn("ACIP handler failed for packet type 0x%02X from WebSocket client %s: %s", packet_type, client_ip,
1505                asciichat_error_string(dispatch_result));
1506     }
1507 
1508     if (alloc_buffer) {
1509       buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1510     }
1511   }
1512 
1513   // Cleanup
1514   tcp_server_remove_client(&server->tcp_server, synthetic_id);
1515   log_debug("WebSocket client %s unregistered (total=%zu)", client_ip,
1516             tcp_server_get_client_count(&server->tcp_server));
1517 
1518   // Transport is owned by WebSocket server, don't destroy it here
1519 
1520   log_info("WebSocket client handler finished for %s", client_ip);
1521 
1522   SAFE_FREE(ctx);
1523 
1524   return NULL;
1525 }






References acip_handle_acds_packet(),
    acip_send_error(), acip_acds_callbacks_t::app_ctx,
    ASCIICHAT_OK, atomic_load_bool(),
    websocket_client_context_t::auth_required, buffer_pool_free(),
    websocket_client_context_t::client_ip, acds_server_t::config,
    crypto_handshake_init(), CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_READY,
    crypto_handshake_server_auth_challenge(),
    crypto_handshake_server_complete(),
    crypto_handshake_server_send_parameters(),
    crypto_handshake_server_start(), private_key_t::ed25519,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT,
    acds_client_data_t::handshake_complete,
    acds_client_data_t::handshake_ctx,
    acds_server_t::identity_public,
    acds_server_t::identity_secret,
    acds_client_data_t::in_multikey_session_create,
    websocket_client_context_t::is_secure,
    acds_client_data_t::joined_session, private_key_t::key,
    public_key_t::key, KEY_TYPE_ED25519,
    packet_header_t::length, log_debug, log_error,
    log_info, log_warn, NET_TO_HOST_U16,
    NET_TO_HOST_U32, packet_receive_via_transport(),
    PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_SESSION_CREATE, PACKET_TYPE_PING,
    PACKET_TYPE_PONG, PACKET_TYPE_PROTOCOL_VERSION,
    acds_client_data_t::registry_id,
    acds_config_t::require_client_identity, tcp_server::running,
    SAFE_CALLOC, SAFE_FREE, SAFE_SNPRINTF,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::server_private_key,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::server_public_key,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::state, acds_server_t::tcp_server,
    tcp_server_add_client(), tcp_server_get_client_count(),
    tcp_server_remove_client(), acds_client_data_t::transport,
    websocket_client_context_t::transport, packet_header_t::type,
    private_key_t::type, public_key_t::type,
    acip_transport::user_data, and
    websocket_client_context_t::user_data.


Referenced by acds_main().
